reviews.review_msg: It is excessively clear that Anne does not care for educating. She leaves classes early frequently and when she stays she mainly shows you TED talks or "teaches" you things that you already have been taught. She also frequently talks down to students. You should avoid Anne at all costs.

Weekly quizzes with only 3 questions, but all easy if you pay attention in lectures and review your notes beforehand. Tests were a bit tricky but not too unfair. Workload was manageable and the work was engaging. Good introduction to ecological theory and taught well! Only gripe is a big emphasis on case studies discussed during lectures.

Great professor! Really took the time to break down the steps on writing an academic paper and helped guide all the students along the journey. The first few weeks of class started off slow, but soon picked up near the end but was always available for providing feedback. Used many different activities to facilitate different learning styles.

Passive aggressive/inauthentic. She built a career on the backs of Indigenous knowledge holders but refuses to acknowledge her racial privileges. She is only looking out for her own interests, and is super self involved. She plays favourites in gross ways, and belittles her own grad students. Will make you feel terrible, don't study with her.
